header.description: Get, add and update data in MySQL

deleteTable.description: Delete an entire table or rows in a table

upsert.description: Insert or update rows in a table

table.description: The table you want to work on

truncate.description: Only removes the table's data and preserves the table's structure

delete.description: Delete the rows that match the 'Select Rows' conditions below. If no selection is made, all rows in the table are deleted.

drop.description: Deletes the table's data and also the table's structure permanently

where.description: If not set, all rows will be selected

column.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

condition.description: The operator to check the column against. When using 'LIKE' operator percent sign ( %) matches zero or more characters, underscore ( _ ) matches any single character.

column.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

condition.description: The operator to check the column against. When using 'LIKE' operator percent sign ( %) matches zero or more characters, underscore ( _ ) matches any single character.

combineConditions.description: How to combine the conditions defined in "Select Rows": AND requires all conditions to be true, OR requires at least one condition to be true

AND.description: Only rows that meet all the conditions are selected

OR.description: Rows that meet at least one condition are selected

connectionTimeoutMillis.description: Number of milliseconds reserved for connecting to the database

connectionLimit.description: Maximum amount of connections to the database, setting high value can lead to performance issues and potential database crashes

queryBatching.description: The way queries should be sent to the database

single.description: A single query for all incoming items

independently.description: Execute one query per incoming item of the run

transaction.description: Execute all queries in a transaction, if a failure occurs, all changes are rolled back

queryReplacement.description: Comma-separated list of the values you want to use as query parameters. You can drag the values from the input panel on the left. More info

outputColumns.description: Choose from the list, or specify IDs using an expression

text.description: Use this if you expect numbers longer than 16 digits (otherwise numbers may be incorrect)

decimalNumbers.description: Whether to output DECIMAL types as numbers instead of strings

priority.description: Ignore any ignorable errors that occur while executing the INSERT statement

LOW_PRIORITY.description: Delays execution of the INSERT until no other clients are reading from the table

HIGH_PRIORITY.description: Overrides the effect of the --low-priority-updates option if the server was started with that option. It also causes concurrent inserts not to be used.

replaceEmptyStrings.description: Whether to replace empty strings with NULL in input, could be useful when data come from spreadsheet

selectDistinct.description: Whether to remove these duplicate rows

detailedOutput.description: Whether to show in output details of the ofexecuted query for each statement, or just confirmation of success

skipOnConflict.description: Whether to skip the row and do not throw error if a unique constraint or exclusion constraint is violated

query.description: The SQL query to execute. You can use n8n expressions and $1, $2, $3, etc to refer to the 'Query Parameters' set in options below.
query.placeholder: e.g. SELECT id, name FROM product WHERE id < 40

dataMode.description: Whether to map node input properties and the table data automatically or manually

autoMapInputData.description: Use when node input properties names exactly match the table column names

defineBelow.description: Set the value for each destination column manually

notice.displayName: In this mode, make sure incoming data fields are named the same as the columns in your table. If needed, use an 'Edit Fields' node before this node to change the field names.

column.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression
Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

returnAll.description: Whether to return all results or only up to a given limit

limit.description: Max number of results to return

column.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression
Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

columnToMatchOn.description: The column to compare when finding the rows to update. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

valueToMatchOn.displayName: Value of Column to Match On
valueToMatchOn.description: Rows with a value in the specified "Column to Match On" that corresponds to the value in this field will be updated. New rows will be created for non-matching items.
